title: AI, Operations & Strategy Associate
key_evidence: Live on Lever (created Jan 2026). London, hybrid, Operations team — location/format fit Ryan well.

MAJOR MISMATCH: despite the 'Operations & Strategy' title, the responsibilities are AI-ENGINEERING/MLOps: 'Design, build, and maintain core AI infrastructure (model hosting, orchestration, data pipelines, evaluation frameworks)'; 'best-practice standards for model deployment, monitoring, observability, security'; 'manage data readiness and governance.' This is an ML platform / AI-infra engineer role, not an operator role. Ryan is explicitly an operator, NOT a software/ML engineer — this is a hard requirements blocker.

Ryan's only matching signal is 'evaluate emerging AI tech/vendors, build-vs-buy, drive experimentation' and 'create reusable tools that democratize AI access' — adjacent to his Claude-Code agent-building, but the bulk of the role requires hands-on model hosting/orchestration/pipeline engineering he cannot evidence.

Domain = healthtech (generalist clinicians delivering specialist care; ex-Oxford doctor + ex-BCG founders; Balderton-backed). Outside Ryan's crypto/Web3+AI-tooling domain; regulated medical data adds compliance ramp.
Founder profile (ex-Oxbridge doctor + ex-BCG) and Balderton backing signal a high analytical/technical bar. No salary published ('competitive based on experience').
biggest_lever: London-hybrid operations-team role with an AI-experimentation/vendor-evaluation slice that lightly touches Ryan's Claude-Code agent work.
biggest_risk: Title is misleading — the actual work is AI-infrastructure/MLOps engineering (model hosting, orchestration, pipelines, eval frameworks) requiring hands-on engineering Ryan does not have; healthtech domain compounds the gap.
apply_verdict: Skip. Despite the attractive 'Operations & Strategy Associate' title, this is an AI-platform/ML-engineering role. Ryan would be screened out on technical requirements.
recency_note: Posted ~141 days / ~4.7 months ago (Lever createdAt epoch 1768834881071 -> 2026-01-19) — very stale -> G=10. Still live on Lever but a nearly-5-month-old req signals either slow hiring or a large accumulated pool. Smaller co (less brand flood than the others) but staleness dominates; callback near-flat 34->32. No applicant count visible.
momentum_signals: FUNDING (NEUTRAL/THIN): Balderton-led seed (£2.2-2.4M, 2023); ~£7M total raised to date. No recent large round surfaced for 2026 — small seed-stage healthtech, not a fresh funding catalyst. Source: Balderton, Crunchbase, businesscloud 2026.

HEADCOUNT (MILD POSITIVE): 76 employees as of 2026-04-30; described as 'rapidly expanding operations across UK and beyond', launching Aerox Sleep Centres — modest organic growth. Source: Crustdata/CB Insights, Balderton careers 2026.

HIRING (NEUTRAL): actively hiring across multiple Lever roles (e.g. Dentist Experience Specialist), but this AI/Ops/Strategy req has sat ~141 days on Lever, suggesting slow fill or selective hiring against a high analytical/technical bar (ex-Oxford-doctor + ex-BCG founders).
Net NEUTRAL (low confidence): no negative flags (no layoffs/down-round), but no recent funding/launch catalyst and a stale req. Thin data => H=50.
